item.title: ops/roles/ directory and .role-manifest.json created without security modes — role assignments world-listable
item.intent: The ops/roles/ directory and .role-manifest.json must be created with mode 0700/0600 — role assignment data must not be listable or readable by other users.
item.description: src/evolve/role/manifest.ts line 79 calls mkdirSync(join(deployDir, MANIFEST_DIR), { recursive: true }) without mode (creates ops/roles/ at 0755). Line 80 calls writeFileSync(path, JSON.stringify(manifest, null, 2) + '\n', 'utf-8') without mode (creates .role-manifest.json at 0644). The manifest stores role definitions and integration-to-role assignments (which integrations have 'admin', 'operator', or 'reader' roles) — exposing this lets an attacker enumerate capability access control assignments. Fix: import chmodSync and DIR_MODE_SECRET, FILE_MODE_SECRET from config/defaults.js; add mode: DIR_MODE_SECRET to mkdirSync; add chmodSync after; change writeFileSync to use FILE_MODE_SECRET; add chmodSync after writeFileSync.
steps: Read src/evolve/role/manifest.ts lines 7-9 — confirm chmodSync is not in the node:fs import and no defaults import exists
Line 7: add chmodSync to the node:fs import — change to: import { chmodSync, existsSync, mkdirSync, readFileSync, writeFileSync } from 'node:fs';
After line 9 (join import): add new import line: import { DIR_MODE_SECRET, FILE_MODE_SECRET } from '../../config/defaults.js';
Line 79: change mkdirSync(join(deployDir, MANIFEST_DIR), { recursive: true }) to mkdirSync(join(deployDir, MANIFEST_DIR), { recursive: true, mode: DIR_MODE_SECRET });
After line 79: add chmodSync(join(deployDir, MANIFEST_DIR), DIR_MODE_SECRET);
Line 80: change writeFileSync(path, JSON.stringify(manifest, null, 2) + '\n', 'utf-8') to writeFileSync(path, JSON.stringify(manifest, null, 2) + '\n', { encoding: 'utf-8', mode: FILE_MODE_SECRET });
Run: npm test -- --testPathPattern='role' and confirm all tests pass
acceptanceCriteria: The ops/roles/ directory is created with mode 0700 — stat confirms no group/other read bits
.role-manifest.json is written with mode 0600 — stat confirms no group/other read bits
All existing role manifest tests pass with no regressions
